behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure scoring logic over provided tokens and configured lists. No real OS/network/subprocess boundary, no external side-effect round trip, and no AI/ML judgment dependency are required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the analyzer function/object directly with fixed positive/negative lexicons and representative reviews; assert exact sentiment outputs at boundary counts.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generation quality/content selection (whether specific phrases are grounded in the input), which a stubbed model can trivially fake. This is a single model-output quality check, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on diverse reviews and score outputs with a rubric: must quote or closely paraphrase concrete source phrases and fail generic template-like explanations.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: The risk is configuration handoff/wiring of tie-break policy across modules (config -> analyzer/service), which unit-only checks can miss even if core scoring logic is tested. No real infra or AI output dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the service with different tie-break configurations and mixed-signal inputs; assert returned sentiment follows the active policy, proving config propagation and analyzer invocation together.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/subprocess/external contract or round-trip side-effect visibility. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because scoring here is keyword-rule based, not model judgment.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly tests semantic intent understanding under sarcasm, i.e., model judgment quality. Deterministic fixtures/mocks would hide the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate real analyzer outputs on a sarcasm-focused set; require explicit negative-intent interpretation tied to sarcastic cues and penalize literal-positive misreads.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration plus semantic consistency where one component is model-generated explanation content. A mock explanation generator can falsely satisfy consistency, so real model output is needed. It is not multiple agentic steps chained across stages, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real combined service path and assert one response contains both fields; validate explanation sentiment direction matches score across positive/negative/neutral cases using real explanation generation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Workflow because behavior text requires single-response consistency, not a multi-step agent pipeline with cross-step model-dependent state evolution.
